definition:
1. with accusative rei, to found as a colony  or new settlement , πόλιν (Herdotus Historicus), etc.:—; Pass., πόλις οἴκισται (Herdotus Historicus) 
2. to people  with new settlers, colonise , χώρην (Herdotus Historicus); νήσους (Thucydides):—;Mid. ὅπη γῆς πύργον οἰκιούμεθα in what part of the world we shall make ourselves  a fenced home, (Euripides) 
3. with accusative pers. to remove, transplant , (Euripides); metaphorically, τὸν μὲν ἀφ᾽ ὑψηλῶν βραχὺν ὤικισεν brought  him from high to low estate, (Euripides):—;Pass. to settle  in a place, (Euripides) (ML)
